subject: Computer Networking
title: Cloud Computing Takes Over
description: A fast breakdown of Cloud Computing Takes Over for builders.

hook.text: In 2006, Amazon launched AWS. Rent a server by the hour instead of buying one. Computing became a utility.

buildup.text: Cloud providers built massive data centers connected by private fiber networks spanning continents.

discovery.text: Startups no longer needed server rooms. They could launch globally on day one with a credit card.

twist.text: The cloud created new networking challenges: latency between regions, data sovereignty, and massive east-west traffic.

climax.text: Today, most internet services run in the cloud. AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud carry a huge share of global traffic.

punchline.text: Cloud computing moved servers off-site but kept the world connected.

quiz.question: What did Amazon launch in 2006 that transformed computing?
